items.title: Ex pro cyclist Rohan Dennis reported for driving while disqualified, ABC understands

children.content: Rohan Dennis was handed a suspended sentence last year for the events leading up to the death of his Olympian wife Melissa Hoskins in December 2023.

gallery.altText: A white sedan on the tray of a tow truck parked in front of a police car

thumbnail.altText: A white sedan on the tray of a tow truck parked in front of a police car

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandparent fined over gun offence, unrelated to 4yo's disappearance

children.content: The grandparent of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont has been fined $10,500 for possession of a gun silencer at the family's Oak Park Station, in South Australia's far north. Police have previously said the offence is not related to his disappearance.

image.altText: A person wearing with shoulder-length blonde hair wearing an Akubra hat and maroon jacket, holding a black handbag

items.title: Line dancing resurgence draws gen Z out to socialise in Adelaide

children.content: It's seen by many as a way to socialise and meet new people in an era where such activities are at a low point due to the ongoing impacts of COVID-19, screen time and social media.

image.altText: A large group of people dance on a dance floor at a pub

items.title: 'Silly thing': Man fined thousands for dumping tyres in heritage-listed area

children.content: A young man who could have paid $115 to discard a trailer load of tyres has been fined thousands for illegally dumping them down a mine shaft in a national heritage-listed area — and ordered to clean it up.

image.altText: A pile of old tyres sitting on an empty lot.

items.title: Fraudsters jailed for stealing millions from 'older and vulnerable' victims

children.content: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

image.altText: A composite image of a bald man and a man with a beard

items.title: Bushies reunite for bikes, beers and banter as record rain lifts spirits

children.content: The trip to Marree in South Australia looked a little different for competitors in this year's annual gymkhana and motorkhana, with the outback blooming after record-breaking rain.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a cowboy hat smiling to the right of screen, leaning against a fence

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a cowboy hat smiling to the right of screen, leaning against a fence

items.title: Adelaide defence lawyer pleads not guilty to sexual offences

children.content: Andrew Graham enters not guilty pleas to two counts of rape and one charge of compelling a person to sexually manipulate an offender.

image.altText: Andrew Graham is speaking outside a sandstone coloured court building

items.title: SA locks in AFL Gather Round until 2029 but no decision on venues

children.content: The AFL's Gather Round will stay in South Australia until 2029 under a new three-year partnership. It will include a parade and a "feature match" but no decision has been made on venues.

image.altText: Children wearing AFL jumpers run through a large blue banner saying GATHER ROUND TO STAY IN SA

items.title: Consumer watchdog warns real estate agents must not misrepresent homes

children.content: Real estate agents selling homes must not misrepresent information about the builder or serious defects, SA's consumer watchdog has said, after eight builders were hit with public warning notices over their conduct in the state.

gallery.altText: A skirting board with a gap to a door frame

thumbnail.altText: A skirting board with a gap to a door frame

items.title: Bakery trains staff to assess counterfeit notes amid rise in use in SA

children.content: An Adelaide business owner who has been targeted by counterfeiters about a dozen times is warning others to be vigilant, as SA Police warn the number of incidents has increased in the state in the past year.

image.altText: A close-up of a hand holding a $50 note and shining an blue UV light on the clear strip.

items.title: Brother and sister from remote SA town receive King's Birthday Honours

children.content: A brother and sister who grew up near the rural town of Lock on the Eyre Peninsula both received honours over the King's Birthday long weekend.

image.altText: A woman stands next to a sign in a rural area. A paddock is behind her.

items.title: Predictive text in 'demonstrable decline' with AI-based language models

children.content: If internet chatter is to be believed, the intuitiveness and efficiency of predictive text and autocorrect have fallen off a cliff in recent years. But while researchers agree there has been a demonstrable decline in quality, the increasing sophistication of what phones can do is also making errors more prominent.

image.altText: a close up of a person holding a mobile phone and texting

items.title: Federal government has contracts worth more than $650m with embattled KPMG

children.content: Parliamentary library data reveals the federal government has almost 300 active contracts with accounting firm KPMG, which is being investigated over claims it misused confidential client information.

image.altText: A large, grey building with a blue and white sign at the top reading KPMG.

items.title: How One Nation could disrupt Australia's progressive stronghold

children.content: The idea that One Nation could come close to the government benches was absurd six months ago, but it is a prospect Victorian Labor MPs and insiders are increasingly discussing.

articles.title: One Nation's Melbourne fundraiser moved over protest concerns

children.content: A One Nation fundraiser in Melbourne has been moved to an undisclosed location after several protest groups vowed to demonstrate outside, the party says.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson Barnaby Joyce

articles.title: Angus Taylor brushes away questions on One Nation preference deal — as it happened

children.content: The opposition leader has brushed away questions on a preference deal with One Nation at the next election.

image.altText: Two men in suits.

gallery.altText: pauline hanson as seen from the crowd as she addresses bbq attendees

thumbnail.altText: pauline hanson as seen from the crowd as she addresses bbq attendees

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: NDIS spending cuts would see 240,000 people exit the scheme, inquiry told

children.content: More than 240,000 people would exit the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) over four years according to modelling as part of a plan to cut spending on the multi-billion-dollar program.

image.altText: Jenny mcalliser appearing at Estimates Committee

items.title: Notorious childcare paedophile loses appeal against non-parole period

children.content: The former childcare worker sexually abused dozens of children at centres for almost two decades.

image.altText: A headshot of Ashley Paul Griffith on a cardboard background.

items.title: Murderer's mum uses ChatGPT to tell court of son's 'caring nature'

children.content: The mother of a man found guilty of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston has told the Supreme Court her son has always been polite and considerate.

image.altText: A close-up selfie of a man in a car.

items.title: Thai king's eldest daughter dies after long illness

children.content: Princess Bajrakitiyabha Narendira Debyavati, who had spent the past three and a half years in a coma due to a heart condition, is being remembered for her efforts to improve the livelihoods of female prisoners and her diplomatic career.

image.altText: A formally dressed middle-aged Thai woman smiles as she leans down to greet members of a crowd.

items.title: What are the rules around using fire pits at home?

children.content: There are rules when it comes to using fire pits and things to be cautious about, including open-air fires being a nuisance to your neighbours.

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: UK minister's shock exit derails Australians' defence visit

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles's visit to the United Kingdom has been thrown into chaos after his British counterpart John Healey resigned hours before they were due to speak.

image.altText: Four people standing and smiling. A member of the King's Guard can be seen in the background.

items.title: Why this fashion designer sells her clothes on social media not shops

children.content: Katharina Mildren's clothes have attracted legions of fans online and she ships items around the world, but there is one place you will not find her garments: in a shop.

image.altText: Young woman with dark hair pulled back away from her face. She is wearing a blue checked dress with puffy sleeves.

items.title: Independence leader vanishes after changing sides to support Indonesia's government

children.content: A key Indigenous leader in West Papua appeared in a documentary criticising an Indonesian government program, but then changed sides and disappeared. Her family suspects foul play.

gallery.altText: Film poster depicting a woman in traditional Papuan clothing with a red cross in the background looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: Film poster depicting a woman in traditional Papuan clothing with a red cross in the background looking at the camera

items.title: Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum join forces thanks to this Australian

children.content: It's possible the ball may not have got rolling on Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's upcoming novel if it wasn't for an Australian connection. Gay will appear at Vivid Sydney in a wide-ranging discussion.

image.altText: Roxane Gay with hair in black and grey braids tied in a bun looking out a window.

items.title: 'Broken promise' or not, Albanese's tax decision helps One Nation

children.content: The prime minister's cavalier breaking of election promises has poured kerosene on the fire under a party that can no longer be called "minor".

image.altText: Prime minister Anthony Albanese

items.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX

children.content: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

image.altText: smoke and flames emerge from a spacex launch in texas

items.title: Government agencies fail first hurdle under AI self-reporting policy

children.content: The ABC can reveal dozens of federal agencies missed mandatory deadlines to disclose how they are currently using AI.

image.altText: A woman's hands type on a laptop keyboard.

items.title: Viral lookout reopens after tourists used yards as 'toilets'

children.content: Lincolns Rock shut after crowds flocked to the site to recreate a photo posted on Instagram of a K-pop star dangling her legs off the sandstone cliff.

gallery.altText: Tourists sit and stand on rock face overlooking the Blue Mountains Valley.
Line of people on a white sandstone rock.

thumbnail.altText: Tourists sit and stand on rock face overlooking the Blue Mountains Valley.
Line of people on a white sandstone rock.

items.title: Greens say premier's answers about legal fees 'paint a far worse picture'

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has provided answers to a series of questions about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fees matter, but the Greens say they "paint a far worse picture of this shameful saga and his role in it".

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff walks into a room at Government House, surrounded by three other men wearing suits.

items.title: Medical care of Floreat double-murderer to be focus of inquest

children.content: The main focus of a coronial inquest into the death of a Perth mother and her daughter will be the medical care their killer Mark Bombara received in the lead up to the double-murder suicide, a coroner reveals.

image.altText: Jennifer and Gretl Petelczyc who were shot dead in their own home by Mark James Bombara.

items.title: Teenager arrested over stabbing death of 21yo man in Tennant Creek

children.content: Police have arrested a 17-year-old boy after a man was fatally stabbed in a street in Tennant Creek overnight.

image.altText: Tennant creek from the top of a hill.

items.title: Amex ordered to compensate man whose ex accessed his data

children.content: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of the findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security, which found widespread technology failures.

image.altText: A modern office building with an American Express sign.

items.title: Couple's $3,000 wedding 'so much fun' as more Australians choose micro-events

children.content: For just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000, Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding.

gallery.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

thumbnail.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion dollar claim figure

children.content: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

image.altText: An industrial plant in the Melbourne suburb of Altona.

items.title: Air India crash plunged victims' families 'into darkness'

children.content: One year on from the fatal Air India crash that claimed 260 lives, families of victims are still waiting for a report into the investigation.

image.altText: A schreenshot of a couple from a video call.
